title: Protocol for manual von Frey
steps: Place clear boxes on top of netting (4 boxes x2 = 8)
Place one animal per box, leave animals in box for 10 minutes to stabilize to the environment
Label your experimental sheet (L/R, X/), Name/Date/Experiment/Rats)

Measure the left paw then the right paw a. The animal is facing away from you - the left paws your left b. Use only the hind paws c. Place the needle in the center of the paw d. Make sure the needle goes through the center of the grid:
Apply the filament just long enough for the animal to respond (1-2 seconds)

Record an X for no response or an 0 for response a. A response is the animal withdrawing the paw upon applying the filament b. Start at the BOTTOM LEFT of the cell on the record sheet for left paw (bottom right for right paw)
IF NO RESPONSE - move down to the next cell, increasing the handle # (example: 5.07) and repeat steps 6-8 a. %s %s
IF RESPONSE - move up to the previous cell, decreasing the handle # (example: 4.93) a. %s %s
Keep going until you have two non-responses followed by two responses in sequential cells a. %s %s
Once you have two non-responses followed by two responses in sequential cells for both left and right paws that animal is done, move on to the next animals
Once all animals are done, return them to their home cage and clean the net and work surface
Return all CLEANED equipment where you found it
creator: Nathan Rizo
